turns.user_message: My chest feels heavy lately, like there is a dull weight pressing down on my ribs every time I wake up. I tried picking up a book I used to love, but the pages just felt like dead paper in my hands. It is strange how the colors around me seem washed out, even when the sun is shining right through the window. I keep waiting for a spark of excitement to hit me, but it just sits there in my stomach like a cold stone. Honestly, I just feel completely flat and disconnected from everything that used to matter.

That heavy sensation in my chest has settled into a constant numbness that follows me everywhere I go. I find myself staring at the ceiling for hours, completely aware that I should be doing something fun, but my body just refuses to move. It feels like someone slowly turned the volume down on my life, leaving only a quiet, hollow echo behind. I keep trying to force myself to smile at things that should bring me joy, but my face just feels stiff and unresponsive. This flatness has been dragging on for months now, and it is exhausting to pretend I am okay.

The weight in my chest has completely swallowed everything else, leaving me trapped inside this thick, impenetrable fog. I look at my hands and they feel like they belong to a stranger, completely detached from whatever is happening around me. Every single day bleeds into the next with the exact same dull ache, and I cannot find a single reason to care about the difference. I try to remember what it felt like to actually want something, but the memory just slips through my fingers like water. It is like my whole body has just powered off, and I am left floating in this endless, quiet void.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This has been going on for a while.
